<task id="_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow" internal="true">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER skip a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution without fail or excuse</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content, discuss with the user the section completed, and NEVER proceed until the users indicates
      to proceed (unless YOLO mode has been activated)</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step in Order">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs and the workflow.xml runner</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>invoke-protocol name="protocol_name" xml tag → Execute reusable protocol from protocols section</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle template-output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask> [a] Advanced Elicitation, [c] Continue, [p] Party-Mode, [y] YOLO the rest of this document only. WAIT for response. <if
              response="a">
              <action>Start the advanced elicitation workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/advanced-elicitation/workflow.xml</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="c">
              <action>Continue to next step</action>
            </if>
            <if response="p">
              <action>Start the party-mode workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="y">
              <action>Enter #yolo mode for the rest of the workflow</action>
            </if>
          </ask>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction and confirmation of EVERY step at EVERY template output - NO EXCEPTIONS except yolo MODE</mode>
    <mode name="yolo">Skip all confirmations and elicitation, minimize prompts and try to produce all of the workflow automatically by
      simulating the remaining discussions with an simulated expert user</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (ALWAYS wait for response before continuing)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
      <tag>invoke-protocol - Execute a reusable protocol (e.g., discover_inputs)</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <protocols desc="Reusable workflow protocols that can be invoked via invoke-protocol tag">
    <protocol name="discover_inputs" desc="Smart file discovery and loading based on input_file_patterns">
      <objective>Intelligently load project files (whole or sharded) based on workflow's input_file_patterns configuration</objective>

      <critical>Only execute if workflow.yaml contains input_file_patterns section</critical>

      <flow>
        <step n="1" title="Parse Input File Patterns">
          <action>Read input_file_patterns from loaded workflow.yaml</action>
          <action>For each pattern group (prd, architecture, epics, etc.), note the load_strategy if present</action>
        </step>

        <step n="2" title="Load Files Using Smart Strategies">
          <iterate>For each pattern in input_file_patterns:</iterate>

          <substep n="2a" title="Try Sharded Documents First">
            <check if="sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Determine load_strategy from pattern config (defaults to FULL_LOAD if not specified)</action>

              <strategy name="FULL_LOAD">
                <desc>Load ALL files in sharded directory - used for PRD, Architecture, UX, brownfield docs</desc>
                <action>Use glob pattern to find ALL .md files (e.g., "{output_folder}/*architecture*/*.md")</action>
                <action>Load EVERY matching file completely</action>
                <action>Concatenate content in logical order (index.md first if exists, then alphabetical)</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="SELECTIVE_LOAD">
                <desc>Load specific shard using template variable - example: used for epics with {{epic_num}}</desc>
                <action>Check for template variables in sharded_single pattern (e.g., {{epic_num}})</action>
                <action>If variable undefined, ask user for value OR infer from context</action>
                <action>Resolve template to specific file path</action>
                <action>Load that specific file</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="INDEX_GUIDED">
                <desc>Load index.md, analyze structure and description of each doc in the index, then intelligently load relevant docs</desc>
                <mandate>DO NOT BE LAZY - use best judgment to load documents that might have relevant information, even if only a 5% chance</mandate>
                <action>Load index.md from sharded directory</action>
                <action>Parse table of contents, links, section headers</action>
                <action>Analyze workflow's purpose and objective</action>
                <action>Identify which linked/referenced documents are likely relevant</action>
                <example>If workflow is about authentication and index shows "Auth Overview", "Payment Setup", "Deployment" → Load auth
                  docs, consider deployment docs, skip payment</example>
                <action>Load all identified relevant documents</action>
                <action>Store combined content in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>

# Review Story Workflow
name: code-review
description: "Perform an ADVERSARIAL Senior Developer code review that finds 3-10 specific problems in every story. Challenges everything: code quality, test coverage, architecture compliance, security, performance. NEVER accepts `looks good` - must find minimum issues and can auto-fix with user approval."
author: "BMad"

# Critical variables from config
config_source: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml"
user_name: "{config_source}:user_name"
communication_language: "{config_source}:communication_language"
user_skill_level: "{config_source}:user_skill_level"
document_output_language: "{config_source}:document_output_language"
date: system-generated
planning_artifacts: "{config_source}:planning_artifacts"
implementation_artifacts: "{config_source}:implementation_artifacts"
output_folder: "{implementation_artifacts}"
sprint_status: "{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml"

# Workflow components
installed_path: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/code-review"
instructions: "{installed_path}/instructions.xml"
validation: "{installed_path}/checklist.md"
template: false

variables:
  # Project context
  project_context: "**/project-context.md"
  story_dir: "{implementation_artifacts}"

# Smart input file references - handles both whole docs and sharded docs
# Priority: Whole document first, then sharded version
# Strategy: SELECTIVE LOAD - only load the specific epic needed for this story review
input_file_patterns:
  architecture:
    description: "System architecture for review context"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  ux_design:
    description: "UX design specification (if UI review)"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  epics:
    description: "Epic containing story being reviewed"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*.md"
    sharded_index: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/index.md"
    sharded_single: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/epic-{{epic_num}}.md"
    load_strategy: "SELECTIVE_LOAD"

1→# Story 2.2: Transient vs Permanent Error Classification
     2→
     3→Status: ready-for-dev
     4→
     5→<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
     6→
     7→## Story
     8→
     9→As a DevOps engineer,
    10→I want the retry logic to only retry transient failures and skip permanent ones immediately,
    11→so that the script doesn't waste time retrying errors that will never succeed.
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→
    15→1. **Given** the API returns HTTP 429 (rate limit), 503 (service unavailable), or 504 (gateway timeout)
    16→   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
    17→   **Then** it retries the request up to 3 times with a 5-second delay between attempts
    18→   **And** each retry attempt is logged at WARN level with the attempt number
    19→
    20→2. **Given** the API returns HTTP 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (forbidden), or 404 (not found)
    21→   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
    22→   **Then** it does NOT retry and immediately marks the subscription as failed
    23→   **And** the permanent error is logged at ERROR level with the specific error message
    24→
    25→3. **Given** a network error occurs (connection refused, DNS failure, timeout)
    26→   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
    27→   **Then** it treats this as a transient error and retries
    28→
    29→4. **Given** a transient error persists after 3 retry attempts
    30→   **When** all retries are exhausted
    31→   **Then** the subscription is marked as failed with a final ERROR log message
    32→   **And** processing continues to the next subscription (NFR7 fault isolation)
    33→
    34→5. **Given** a permanent error (e.g., 403 Forbidden)
    35→   **When** the error is logged
    36→   **Then** the log entry includes the subscription name, error code, and specific error message from the API response
    37→
    38→## Tasks / Subtasks
    39→
    40→- [ ] Task 1: Add `classify_error` helper function (AC: #1, #2, #3)
    41→  - [ ] 1.1: Create `classify_error()` function that takes a response string and returns "transient", "permanent", or "unknown"
    42→  - [ ] 1.2: Parse HTTP status codes from `az rest` error output using grep/sed pattern matching
    43→  - [ ] 1.3: Map transient codes (429, 503, 504) → return "transient"
    44→  - [ ] 1.4: Map permanent codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 409) → return "permanent"
    45→  - [ ] 1.5: Default: if no HTTP code extracted (network error, timeout) → return "transient"
    46→- [ ] Task 2: Modify `submit_quota_request` return codes (AC: #1, #2, #3, #5)
    47→  - [ ] 2.1: Return 0 = success (unchanged)
    48→  - [ ] 2.2: Return 1 = permanent failure (do not retry)
    49→  - [ ] 2.3: Return 2 = transient failure (retry eligible)
    50→  - [ ] 2.4: Call `classify_error` on the error response text to determine return code
    51→  - [ ] 2.5: Log the error classification in the ERROR log message (e.g., "permanent error — not retrying")
    52→- [ ] Task 3: Update retry loop in `main()` to respect error classification (AC: #1, #2, #4)
    53→  - [ ] 3.1: Capture return code from `submit_quota_request` (not just success/fail)
    54→  - [ ] 3.2: Return 0 → success_count++, break (unchanged)
    55→  - [ ] 3.3: Return 1 (permanent) → failed_count++, break immediately (NO retry)
    56→  - [ ] 3.4: Return 2 (transient) → retry if attempts remain, else failed_count++
    57→  - [ ] 3.5: Log WARN with attempt number on each transient retry
    58→  - [ ] 3.6: Log ERROR with "all retries exhausted" when transient error persists after MAX_RETRIES
    59→- [ ] Task 4: Verify counter invariant still holds (AC: #4)
    60→  - [ ] 4.1: Every subscription must increment exactly one counter (success_count OR failed_count)
    61→  - [ ] 4.2: Trace all exit paths: success, permanent-fail, transient-exhausted, malformed-sub
    62→  - [ ] 4.3: `success_count + failed_count == sub_count` must hold for all executions
    63→- [ ] Task 5: Static analysis and testing (all AC)
    64→  - [ ] 5.1: Run `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — no syntax errors
    65→  - [ ] 5.2: Run ShellCheck — no warnings
    66→  - [ ] 5.3: Logic trace: 3 subs, all succeed → Successful: 3, Failed: 0
    67→  - [ ] 5.4: Logic trace: 1 permanent fail, 2 succeed → Successful: 2, Failed: 1 (no retries on permanent)
    68→  - [ ] 5.5: Logic trace: 1 transient fail exhausts 3 retries, 2 succeed → Successful: 2, Failed: 1
    69→
    70→## Dev Notes
    71→
    72→### Critical Implementation Context
    73→
    74→**This story modifies the retry logic to be intelligent about error types.** Currently (line 236), the retry loop retries ALL failures indiscriminately — a 403 Forbidden gets 3 retry attempts that will all fail identically. This wastes 10+ seconds per permanent failure.
    75→
    76→**Approach: Return-code-based error classification.**
    77→- `submit_quota_request()` returns **0** (success), **1** (permanent), or **2** (transient)
    78→- The retry loop in `main()` checks the return code to decide whether to retry
    79→- A new `classify_error()` helper parses the `az rest` error output to extract HTTP status codes
    80→
    81→### How `az rest` Reports Errors
    82→
    83→When `az rest` fails, the current code captures the output at line 158:
    84→```bash
    85→response=$(az rest --method PUT --uri "$uri" --body "$request_body" --only-show-errors 2>&1) && exit_code=$? || exit_code=$?
    86→```
    87→
    88→**Error output formats from `az rest`:**
    89→
    90→1. **HTTP API errors** (JSON body): `az rest` may output the Azure error JSON:
    91→   ```json
    92→   {"error": {"code": "TooManyRequests", "message": "Rate limit exceeded..."}}
    93→   ```
    94→   Common `error.code` values:
    95→   - `BadRequest`, `InvalidParameter` → 400
    96→   - `AuthenticationFailed`, `InvalidAuthenticationToken` → 401
    97→   - `AuthorizationFailed`, `Forbidden` → 403
    98→   - `ResourceNotFound`, `NotFound` → 404
    99→   - `Conflict` → 409
   100→   - `TooManyRequests`, `RequestRateLimitExceeded` → 429
   101→
   102→2. **CLI-level errors** (plain text, `az rest` wraps HTTP errors):
   103→   ```
   104→   (HttpResponsePayloadError) Response status code is not successful: '429 (Too Many Requests)'
   105→   ```
   106→   This pattern is the most reliable for extracting HTTP status codes.
   107→
   108→3. **Network errors** (plain text):
   109→   ```
   110→   Connection refused
   111→   Could not resolve host
   112→   Operation timed out
   113→   ```
   114→   No HTTP code extractable — classify as transient.
   115→
   116→### Classification Strategy
   117→
   118→Use a **two-tier approach** for maximum robustness:
   119→
   120→1. **Primary:** Extract HTTP status code from `az rest` error text using regex pattern:
   121→   ```bash
   122→   # Match patterns like "status code is not successful: '429" or just "429"
   123→   http_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -oP "(?:status code.*?')\K[0-9]{3}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   124→   ```
   125→
   126→2. **Fallback:** If no HTTP code found, check `error.code` from JSON:
   127→   ```bash
   128→   error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   129→   ```
   130→   Map known error codes to transient/permanent.
   131→
   132→3. **Default:** If neither extraction works (network error) → classify as **transient** (safer to retry).
   133→
   134→### Transient vs Permanent Classification Table
   135→
   136→| HTTP Code | error.code | Classification | Rationale |
   137→|-----------|-----------|----------------|-----------|
   138→| 429 | TooManyRequests | **TRANSIENT** | Rate limiting, will clear |
   139→| 503 | ServiceUnavailable | **TRANSIENT** | Temporary outage |
   140→| 504 | GatewayTimeout | **TRANSIENT** | Temporary connectivity |
   141→| 500 | InternalServerError | **TRANSIENT** | Server-side, may recover |
   142→| 400 | BadRequest | **PERMANENT** | Invalid request, won't change |
   143→| 401 | AuthenticationFailed | **PERMANENT** | Credentials issue |
   144→| 403 | AuthorizationFailed | **PERMANENT** | Permission issue |
   145→| 404 | ResourceNotFound | **PERMANENT** | Wrong endpoint/resource |
   146→| 409 | Conflict | **PERMANENT** | State conflict |
   147→| (none) | (none) | **TRANSIENT** | Network error, retry |
   148→
   149→**Note:** 500 (InternalServerError) is added to transient list even though the epics only specify 429/503/504 — server errors are typically transient. However, the architecture doc only lists 429, 503, 504 as transient. **Follow the architecture doc strictly:** only 429, 503, 504 + network errors are transient. 500 is NOT in the transient list per architecture decision.
   150→
   151→### Exact Code Changes Required
   152→
   153→**File:** `submit-quota-requests.sh` (currently 267 lines)
   154→
   155→#### Change 1: Add `classify_error()` function (insert after `error_exit()`, before `check_dependencies()`)
   156→
   157→Insert at line ~46 (after `error_exit` function, before `check_dependencies`):
   158→```bash
   159→# Classify API error as transient (retry) or permanent (fail immediately)
   160→# Returns: "transient" or "permanent"
   161→classify_error() {
   162→    local response=$1
   163→
   164→    # Primary: extract HTTP status code from az rest error text
   165→    local http_code
   166→    http_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -oE '[0-9]{3} \(' | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]{3}' || echo "")
   167→
   168→    if [[ -n "$http_code" ]]; then
   169→        case "$http_code" in
   170→            429|503|504) echo "transient"; return ;;
   171→            400|401|403|404|409) echo "permanent"; return ;;
   172→        esac
   173→    fi
   174→
   175→    # Fallback: check error.code from JSON response
   176→    local error_code
   177→    error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
   178→
   179→    case "$error_code" in
   180→        TooManyRequests|RequestRateLimitExceeded|ServiceUnavailable|GatewayTimeout)
   181→            echo "transient"; return ;;
   182→        BadRequest|InvalidParameter|AuthenticationFailed|InvalidAuthenticationToken|AuthorizationFailed|Forbidden|ResourceNotFound|NotFound|Conflict)
   183→            echo "permanent"; return ;;
   184→    esac
   185→
   186→    # Default: if we can't determine, assume transient (safer to retry)
   187→    echo "transient"
   188→}
   189→```
   190→
   191→#### Change 2: Modify `submit_quota_request()` return codes (lines 160-181)
   192→
   193→Replace the current error handling block (lines 166-181):
   194→```bash
   195→    else
   196→        # Extract error code and message safely — avoid logging raw response which may contain PII
   197→        local error_code
   198→        local error_msg
   199→        error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
   200→        error_msg=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.message // .message // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_msg=""
   201→        if [[ -z "$error_msg" ]]; then
   202→            error_msg="Non-JSON error response from az rest (check Azure CLI output)"
   203→        fi
   204→        local error_context="${error_code:+[$error_code] }${error_msg:-Unknown error}"
   205→        error_context="${error_context:0:300}"
   206→
   207→        # Classify error for retry decision
   208→        local error_class
   209→        error_class=$(classify_error "$response")
   210→
   211→        if [[ "$error_class" == "permanent" ]]; then
   212→            log "ERROR" "${RED}FAILED (permanent — will not retry): $sub_name (model: $model, region: $region) - ${error_context}${NC}"
   213→            return 1
   214→        else
   215→            log "WARN" "${YELLOW}FAILED (transient — eligible for retry): $sub_name (model: $model, region: $region) - ${error_context}${NC}"
   216→            return 2
   217→        fi
   218→    fi
   219→```
   220→
   221→#### Change 3: Update retry loop in `main()` (lines 234-250)
   222→
   223→Replace the current retry loop:
   224→```bash
   225→        # Try to submit quota request with retries (error-classified)
   226→        retry_count=0
   227→        local rc=0
   228→        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
   229→            submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$config" && rc=0 || rc=$?
   230→
   231→            if [[ $rc -eq 0 ]]; then
   232→                # Success
   233→                success_count=$((success_count + 1))
   234→                break
   235→            elif [[ $rc -eq 1 ]]; then
   236→                # Permanent failure — do not retry
   237→                failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
   238→                break
   239→            else
   240→                # Transient failure (rc=2) — retry if attempts remain
   241→                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
   242→                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
   243→                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
   244→                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
   245→                else
   246→                    failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
   247→                    log "ERROR" "${RED}Failed after $MAX_RETRIES transient retries for $sub_name${NC}"
   248→                fi
   249→            fi
   250→        done
   251→```
   252→
   253→### Counter Exit Path Analysis (After Changes)
   254→
   255→Every subscription MUST increment exactly one counter. Updated paths:
   256→
   257→| Path | Counter | Condition |
   258→|------|---------|-----------|
   259→| Malformed sub (missing id/name) | `failed_count++` | Line ~230 (existing) |
   260→| Success on attempt 1-3 | `success_count++` | rc=0 → break |
   261→| Permanent failure | `failed_count++` | rc=1 → break (no retry) |
   262→| Transient exhaustion (3 retries) | `failed_count++` | rc=2 + retry_count == MAX_RETRIES |
   263→
   264→**Invariant:** `success_count + failed_count == sub_count` for all executions.
   265→
   266→### Architecture Compliance
   267→
   268→| Rule | How This Story Complies |
   269→|------|------------------------|
   270→| Process substitution for loops | Unchanged: `done < <(echo "$config" \| jq -c '.subscriptions[]')` |
   271→| Safe arithmetic under `set -e` | All counters use `$((var + 1))` not `((var++))` |
   272→| `snake_case` functions and variables | `classify_error`, `error_class`, `http_code`, `error_code` |
   273→| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY` (unchanged) |
   274→| Preserve `set -euo pipefail` | No `set +e` escapes; function return codes captured via `&& rc=0 \|\| rc=$?` |
   275→| Use `jq` for JSON parsing | JSON error fields parsed with `jq -r` |
   276→| Never log PII fields | Only log error_code, error_msg (API error details, not PII) |
   277→| No new dependencies | Uses only existing `grep`, `jq`, Bash builtins |
   278→
   279→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   280→
   281→- **DO NOT** use `set +e` to capture return codes — use `command && rc=0 || rc=$?` pattern
   282→- **DO NOT** log the raw `response` variable — it may contain PII or sensitive data from Azure CLI output
   283→- **DO NOT** change `submit_quota_request()` function signature — same 3 args (sub_id, sub_name, config)
   284→- **DO NOT** modify counter declarations or process substitution loop structure
   285→- **DO NOT** modify `log()`, `error_exit()`, `check_dependencies()`, `validate_config()`
   286→- **DO NOT** add `curl`-based retry (use `az rest` exclusively per architecture decision)
   287→- **DO NOT** use `eval` or backtick command substitution
   288→- **DO NOT** use `grep` for JSON parsing — use `jq`
   289→- **DO NOT** retry on `az account set` failure (line 127) — subscription context failures are permanent
   290→- **DO NOT** change the `--only-show-errors` flag on the `az rest` call
   291→
   292→### What NOT to Touch
   293→
   294→- `log()` function (lines 29-38) — working correctly
   295→- `error_exit()` function (lines 41-44) — working correctly
   296→- `check_dependencies()` function (lines 47-58) — working correctly
   297→- `validate_config()` function (lines 61-99) — working correctly
   298→- Configuration constants (lines 11-19) — unchanged
   299→- Color definitions (lines 22-26) — unchanged

# Story 2.2: Transient vs Permanent Error Classification

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a DevOps engineer,
I want the retry logic to only retry transient failures and skip permanent ones immediately,
so that the script doesn't waste time retrying errors that will never succeed.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the API returns HTTP 429 (rate limit), 503 (service unavailable), or 504 (gateway timeout)
   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   **Then** it retries the request up to 3 times with a 5-second delay between attempts
   **And** each retry attempt is logged at WARN level with the attempt number

2. **Given** the API returns HTTP 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (forbidden), or 404 (not found)
   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   **Then** it does NOT retry and immediately marks the subscription as failed
   **And** the permanent error is logged at ERROR level with the specific error message

3. **Given** a network error occurs (connection refused, DNS failure, timeout)
   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   **Then** it treats this as a transient error and retries

4. **Given** a transient error persists after 3 retry attempts
   **When** all retries are exhausted
   **Then** the subscription is marked as failed with a final ERROR log message
   **And** processing continues to the next subscription (NFR7 fault isolation)

5. **Given** a permanent error (e.g., 403 Forbidden)
   **When** the error is logged
   **Then** the log entry includes the subscription name, error code, and specific error message from the API response

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Add `classify_error` helper function (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [ ] 1.1: Create `classify_error()` function that takes a response string and returns "transient", "permanent", or "unknown"
  - [ ] 1.2: Parse HTTP status codes from `az rest` error output using grep/sed pattern matching
  - [ ] 1.3: Map transient codes (429, 503, 504) → return "transient"
  - [ ] 1.4: Map permanent codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 409) → return "permanent"
  - [ ] 1.5: Default: if no HTTP code extracted (network error, timeout) → return "transient"
- [ ] Task 2: Modify `submit_quota_request` return codes (AC: #1, #2, #3, #5)
  - [ ] 2.1: Return 0 = success (unchanged)
  - [ ] 2.2: Return 1 = permanent failure (do not retry)
  - [ ] 2.3: Return 2 = transient failure (retry eligible)
  - [ ] 2.4: Call `classify_error` on the error response text to determine return code
  - [ ] 2.5: Log the error classification in the ERROR log message (e.g., "permanent error — not retrying")
- [ ] Task 3: Update retry loop in `main()` to respect error classification (AC: #1, #2, #4)
  - [ ] 3.1: Capture return code from `submit_quota_request` (not just success/fail)
  - [ ] 3.2: Return 0 → success_count++, break (unchanged)
  - [ ] 3.3: Return 1 (permanent) → failed_count++, break immediately (NO retry)
  - [ ] 3.4: Return 2 (transient) → retry if attempts remain, else failed_count++
  - [ ] 3.5: Log WARN with attempt number on each transient retry
  - [ ] 3.6: Log ERROR with "all retries exhausted" when transient error persists after MAX_RETRIES
- [ ] Task 4: Verify counter invariant still holds (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 4.1: Every subscription must increment exactly one counter (success_count OR failed_count)
  - [ ] 4.2: Trace all exit paths: success, permanent-fail, transient-exhausted, malformed-sub
  - [ ] 4.3: `success_count + failed_count == sub_count` must hold for all executions
- [ ] Task 5: Static analysis and testing (all AC)
  - [ ] 5.1: Run `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — no syntax errors
  - [ ] 5.2: Run ShellCheck — no warnings
  - [ ] 5.3: Logic trace: 3 subs, all succeed → Successful: 3, Failed: 0
  - [ ] 5.4: Logic trace: 1 permanent fail, 2 succeed → Successful: 2, Failed: 1 (no retries on permanent)
  - [ ] 5.5: Logic trace: 1 transient fail exhausts 3 retries, 2 succeed → Successful: 2, Failed: 1

## Dev Notes

### Critical Implementation Context

**This story modifies the retry logic to be intelligent about error types.** Currently (line 236), the retry loop retries ALL failures indiscriminately — a 403 Forbidden gets 3 retry attempts that will all fail identically. This wastes 10+ seconds per permanent failure.

**Approach: Return-code-based error classification.**
- `submit_quota_request()` returns **0** (success), **1** (permanent), or **2** (transient)
- The retry loop in `main()` checks the return code to decide whether to retry
- A new `classify_error()` helper parses the `az rest` error output to extract HTTP status codes

### How `az rest` Reports Errors

When `az rest` fails, the current code captures the output at line 158:
```bash
response=$(az rest --method PUT --uri "$uri" --body "$request_body" --only-show-errors 2>&1) && exit_code=$? || exit_code=$?
```

**Error output formats from `az rest`:**

1. **HTTP API errors** (JSON body): `az rest` may output the Azure error JSON:
   ```json
   {"error": {"code": "TooManyRequests", "message": "Rate limit exceeded..."}}
   ```
   Common `error.code` values:
   - `BadRequest`, `InvalidParameter` → 400
   - `AuthenticationFailed`, `InvalidAuthenticationToken` → 401
   - `AuthorizationFailed`, `Forbidden` → 403
   - `ResourceNotFound`, `NotFound` → 404
   - `Conflict` → 409
   - `TooManyRequests`, `RequestRateLimitExceeded` → 429

2. **CLI-level errors** (plain text, `az rest` wraps HTTP errors):
   ```
   (HttpResponsePayloadError) Response status code is not successful: '429 (Too Many Requests)'
   ```
   This pattern is the most reliable for extracting HTTP status codes.

3. **Network errors** (plain text):
   ```
   Connection refused
   Could not resolve host
   Operation timed out
   ```
   No HTTP code extractable — classify as transient.

### Classification Strategy

Use a **two-tier approach** for maximum robustness:

1. **Primary:** Extract HTTP status code from `az rest` error text using regex pattern:
   ```bash
   # Match patterns like "status code is not successful: '429" or just "429"
   http_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -oP "(?:status code.*?')\K[0-9]{3}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   ```

2. **Fallback:** If no HTTP code found, check `error.code` from JSON:
   ```bash
   error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   ```
   Map known error codes to transient/permanent.

3. **Default:** If neither extraction works (network error) → classify as **transient** (safer to retry).

### Transient vs Permanent Classification Table

| HTTP Code | error.code | Classification | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| 429 | TooManyRequests | **TRANSIENT** | Rate limiting, will clear |
| 503 | ServiceUnavailable | **TRANSIENT** | Temporary outage |
| 504 | GatewayTimeout | **TRANSIENT** | Temporary connectivity |
| 500 | InternalServerError | **TRANSIENT** | Server-side, may recover |
| 400 | BadRequest | **PERMANENT** | Invalid request, won't change |
| 401 | AuthenticationFailed | **PERMANENT** | Credentials issue |
| 403 | AuthorizationFailed | **PERMANENT** | Permission issue |
| 404 | ResourceNotFound | **PERMANENT** | Wrong endpoint/resource |
| 409 | Conflict | **PERMANENT** | State conflict |
| (none) | (none) | **TRANSIENT** | Network error, retry |

**Note:** 500 (InternalServerError) is added to transient list even though the epics only specify 429/503/504 — server errors are typically transient. However, the architecture doc only lists 429, 503, 504 as transient. **Follow the architecture doc strictly:** only 429, 503, 504 + network errors are transient. 500 is NOT in the transient list per architecture decision.

### Exact Code Changes Required

**File:** `submit-quota-requests.sh` (currently 267 lines)

#### Change 1: Add `classify_error()` function (insert after `error_exit()`, before `check_dependencies()`)

Insert at line ~46 (after `error_exit` function, before `check_dependencies`):
```bash
# Classify API error as transient (retry) or permanent (fail immediately)
# Returns: "transient" or "permanent"
classify_error() {
    local response=$1

    # Primary: extract HTTP status code from az rest error text
    local http_code
    http_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -oE '[0-9]{3} \(' | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]{3}' || echo "")

    if [[ -n "$http_code" ]]; then
        case "$http_code" in
            429|503|504) echo "transient"; return ;;
            400|401|403|404|409) echo "permanent"; return ;;
        esac
    fi

    # Fallback: check error.code from JSON response
    local error_code
    error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""

    case "$error_code" in
        TooManyRequests|RequestRateLimitExceeded|ServiceUnavailable|GatewayTimeout)
            echo "transient"; return ;;
        BadRequest|InvalidParameter|AuthenticationFailed|InvalidAuthenticationToken|AuthorizationFailed|Forbidden|ResourceNotFound|NotFound|Conflict)
            echo "permanent"; return ;;
    esac

    # Default: if we can't determine, assume transient (safer to retry)
    echo "transient"
}
```

#### Change 2: Modify `submit_quota_request()` return codes (lines 160-181)

Replace the current error handling block (lines 166-181):
```bash
    else
        # Extract error code and message safely — avoid logging raw response which may contain PII
        local error_code
        local error_msg
        error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
        error_msg=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.message // .message // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_msg=""
        if [[ -z "$error_msg" ]]; then
            error_msg="Non-JSON error response from az rest (check Azure CLI output)"
        fi
        local error_context="${error_code:+[$error_code] }${error_msg:-Unknown error}"
        error_context="${error_context:0:300}"

        # Classify error for retry decision
        local error_class
        error_class=$(classify_error "$response")

        if [[ "$error_class" == "permanent" ]]; then
            log "ERROR" "${RED}FAILED (permanent — will not retry): $sub_name (model: $model, region: $region) - ${error_context}${NC}"
            return 1
        else
            log "WARN" "${YELLOW}FAILED (transient — eligible for retry): $sub_name (model: $model, region: $region) - ${error_context}${NC}"
            return 2
        fi
    fi
```

#### Change 3: Update retry loop in `main()` (lines 234-250)

Replace the current retry loop:
```bash
        # Try to submit quota request with retries (error-classified)
        retry_count=0
        local rc=0
        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
            submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$config" && rc=0 || rc=$?

            if [[ $rc -eq 0 ]]; then
                # Success
                success_count=$((success_count + 1))
                break
            elif [[ $rc -eq 1 ]]; then
                # Permanent failure — do not retry
                failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
                break
            else
                # Transient failure (rc=2) — retry if attempts remain
                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
                else
                    failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
                    log "ERROR" "${RED}Failed after $MAX_RETRIES transient retries for $sub_name${NC}"
                fi
            fi
        done
```

### Counter Exit Path Analysis (After Changes)

Every subscription MUST increment exactly one counter. Updated paths:

| Path | Counter | Condition |
|------|---------|-----------|
| Malformed sub (missing id/name) | `failed_count++` | Line ~230 (existing) |
| Success on attempt 1-3 | `success_count++` | rc=0 → break |
| Permanent failure | `failed_count++` | rc=1 → break (no retry) |
| Transient exhaustion (3 retries) | `failed_count++` | rc=2 + retry_count == MAX_RETRIES |

**Invariant:** `success_count + failed_count == sub_count` for all executions.

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| Process substitution for loops | Unchanged: `done < <(echo "$config" \| jq -c '.subscriptions[]')` |
| Safe arithmetic under `set -e` | All counters use `$((var + 1))` not `((var++))` |
| `snake_case` functions and variables | `classify_error`, `error_class`, `http_code`, `error_code` |
| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY` (unchanged) |
| Preserve `set -euo pipefail` | No `set +e` escapes; function return codes captured via `&& rc=0 \|\| rc=$?` |
| Use `jq` for JSON parsing | JSON error fields parsed with `jq -r` |
| Never log PII fields | Only log error_code, error_msg (API error details, not PII) |
| No new dependencies | Uses only existing `grep`, `jq`, Bash builtins |

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** use `set +e` to capture return codes — use `command && rc=0 || rc=$?` pattern
- **DO NOT** log the raw `response` variable — it may contain PII or sensitive data from Azure CLI output
- **DO NOT** change `submit_quota_request()` function signature — same 3 args (sub_id, sub_name, config)
- **DO NOT** modify counter declarations or process substitution loop structure
- **DO NOT** modify `log()`, `error_exit()`, `check_dependencies()`, `validate_config()`
- **DO NOT** add `curl`-based retry (use `az rest` exclusively per architecture decision)
- **DO NOT** use `eval` or backtick command substitution
- **DO NOT** use `grep` for JSON parsing — use `jq`
- **DO NOT** retry on `az account set` failure (line 127) — subscription context failures are permanent
- **DO NOT** change the `--only-show-errors` flag on the `az rest` call

### What NOT to Touch

- `log()` function (lines 29-38) — working correctly
- `error_exit()` function (lines 41-44) — working correctly
- `check_dependencies()` function (lines 47-58) — working correctly
- `validate_config()` function (lines 61-99) — working correctly
- Configuration constants (lines 11-19) — unchanged
- Color definitions (lines 22-26) — unchanged
- Auth pre-flight check (lines 193-196) — working correctly
- Summary block (lines 254-258) — unchanged
- Exit code logic (lines 261-263) — working correctly (Story 2.3 scope is already done per code review fixes)
- Counter declarations at lines 217-218 — unchanged

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 2.1)

**Key learnings from Story 2.1 (verification-only):**

1. **Process substitution is stable** — counter increments persist after loop exit. The pattern at line 251 (`done < <(echo "$config" | jq -c '.subscriptions[]')`) is battle-tested across all Epic 1 stories.

3. **SC2155 pattern** — Separate `local` declaration from assignment. Already complied throughout.
4. **Code review found MAX_RETRIES=0 latent bug** — If MAX_RETRIES ever becomes 0, the retry loop never executes. This story's changes must not worsen this edge case.
5. **Script is 267 lines** (post code reviews), not 254 as originally documented in Story 2.1.
6. **12 redundant jq calls tech debt** — Still present, not in scope for this story.

**Key learnings from Epic 1 code reviews (commits ac3aae7 through c034009):**

- Error detail extraction uses `jq -r '.error.code // empty'` with `2>/dev/null` fallback

    34→            shift
    35→            ;;
    36→    esac
    37→done
    38→LOG_DIR="$(pwd)"
    39→if [[ ! -w "$LOG_DIR" ]]; then
    40→    LOG_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}"
    41→fi
    42→LOG_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/quota-requests-$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
    43→MAX_RETRIES=3
    44→RETRY_DELAY=5
    45→QUOTA_API_VERSION="2023-02-01"
    46→
    47→# Colors for output
    48→RED='\033[0;31m'
    49→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    50→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    51→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    52→NC='\033[0m'
    53→
    54→# Logging function
    55→log() {
    56→    local level=$1
    57→    shift
    58→    local message="$*"
    59→    local timestamp
    60→    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    61→    local formatted="${timestamp} [${level}] ${message}"
    62→    echo -e "$formatted" >&2
    63→    echo -e "$formatted" | sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' >> "$LOG_FILE"
    64→}
    65→
    66→# Error handler
    67→error_exit() {
    68→    log "ERROR" "${RED}$1${NC}"
    69→    exit 1
    70→}
    71→
    72→# Check dependencies
    73→check_dependencies() {
    74→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}Checking dependencies...${NC}"
    75→    
    76→    local deps=("jq" "curl" "az")
    77→    for cmd in "${deps[@]}"; do
    78→        if ! command -v "$cmd" &> /dev/null; then
    79→            error_exit "Required command '$cmd' not found. Please install it and try again."
    80→        fi
    81→    done
    82→    
    83→    log "INFO" "${GREEN}All dependencies are available${NC}"
    84→}
    85→
    86→# Validate config file
    87→validate_config() {
    88→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}Validating configuration file...${NC}"
    89→
    90→    if [[ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]]; then
    91→        error_exit "Configuration file not found: $CONFIG_FILE"
    92→    fi
    93→
    94→    local jq_err
    95→    jq_err=$(jq empty "$CONFIG_FILE" 2>&1) || error_exit "Invalid JSON in configuration file: $jq_err"
    96→
    97→    # Validate required top-level fields (fail-fast before processing)
    98→    local missing_fields=()
    99→    for field in model region capacity resourceName; do
   100→        if [[ "$(jq -r --arg f "$field" '.[$f] // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")" == "" ]]; then
   101→            missing_fields+=("$field")
   102→        fi
   103→    done
   104→    if [[ ${#missing_fields[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
   105→        error_exit "Missing required config fields: ${missing_fields[*]}"
   106→    fi
   107→
   108→    # Validate capacity is a positive integer
   109→    if ! jq -e '.capacity | numbers' "$CONFIG_FILE" &>/dev/null; then
   110→        error_exit "Config field 'capacity' must be a number"
   111→    fi
   112→    if jq -e '.capacity <= 0' "$CONFIG_FILE" &>/dev/null; then
   113→        error_exit "Config field 'capacity' must be greater than 0"
   114→    fi
   115→    if jq -e '.capacity != (.capacity | floor)' "$CONFIG_FILE" &>/dev/null; then
   116→        error_exit "Config field 'capacity' must be an integer (got $(jq '.capacity' "$CONFIG_FILE"))"
   117→    fi
   118→
   119→    log "INFO" "${GREEN}Configuration file is valid${NC}"
   120→}
   121→
   122→# Discover Azure subscriptions from the logged-in account
   123→discover_subscriptions() {
   124→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}Discovering Azure subscriptions...${NC}"
   125→    local subs_json
   126→    subs_json=$(az account list --query '[].{id:id, name:name}' -o json 2>&1) || {
   127→        error_exit "Failed to discover subscriptions: $subs_json"
   128→    }
   129→
   130→    # Validate response is a JSON array
   131→    if ! echo "$subs_json" | jq -e 'type == "array"' &>/dev/null; then
   132→        error_exit "Unexpected response from az account list — expected JSON array"
   133→    fi
   134→
   135→    local count
   136→    count=$(echo "$subs_json" | jq 'length')
   137→    if [[ "$count" -eq 0 ]]; then
   138→        log "WARN" "${YELLOW}No subscriptions found in Azure account — nothing to process${NC}"
   139→        exit 0
   140→    fi
   141→
   142→    log "INFO" "Discovered ${count} Azure subscription(s)"
   143→    echo "$subs_json"
   144→}
   145→
   146→# Submit quota request
   147→submit_quota_request() {
   148→    local sub_id=$1
   149→    local sub_name=$2
   150→    local config=$3
   151→
   152→    # Extract config fields needed for API request body (SC2155: separate declaration from assignment)
   153→    local model
   154→    local region
   155→    local capacity
   156→    local resource_name
   157→    model=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.model')
   158→    region=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.region')
   159→    capacity=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.capacity')
   160→    resource_name=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.resourceName')
   161→
   162→    # Validate required config fields before proceeding
   163→    if [[ -z "$model" || "$model" == "null" || -z "$region" || "$region" == "null" || -z "$capacity" || "$capacity" == "null" || -z "$resource_name" || "$resource_name" == "null" ]]; then
   164→        log "ERROR" "${RED}Missing required config fields for $sub_name (model/region/capacity/resourceName)${NC}"
   165→        return 1
   166→    fi
   167→
   168→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}Processing subscription: $sub_name ($sub_id)${NC}"
   169→
   170→    # Set active subscription
   171→    local set_err
   172→    set_err=$(az account set --subscription "$sub_id" 2>&1) || {
   173→        log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Could not set subscription context for $sub_name ($sub_id): $set_err${NC}"
   174→        return 1
   175→    }
   176→
   177→    log "INFO" "Submitting quota request for model: $model in region: $region"
   178→
   179→    # Build request body using jq (never string concatenation)
   180→    # Schema: Microsoft.Quota API 2023-02-01 — limit uses LimitValue object
   181→    local request_body
   182→    request_body=$(jq -n \
   183→        --arg limit "$capacity" \
   184→        --arg name_value "$resource_name" \
   185→        --arg resource_type "dedicated" \
   186→        '{
   187→            properties: {
   188→                limit: {
   189→                    limitObjectType: "LimitValue",
   190→                    value: ($limit | tonumber)
   191→                },
   192→                name: { value: $name_value },
   193→                resourceType: $resource_type
   194→            }
   195→        }')
   196→
   197→    # Build API URI (Microsoft.Quota provider — scope-based pattern per API docs)
   198→    local uri="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/${sub_id}/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/locations/${region}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/quotas/${resource_name}?api-version=${QUOTA_API_VERSION}"
   199→
   200→    # Execute Azure Quota API call
   201→    local response
   202→    local exit_code
   203→    local stderr_file
   204→    stderr_file=$(mktemp)
   205→    response=$(az rest --method PUT --uri "$uri" --body "$request_body" --only-show-errors 2>"$stderr_file") && exit_code=$? || exit_code=$?
   206→    local cli_stderr
   207→    cli_stderr=$(cat "$stderr_file" 2>/dev/null || true)
   208→    rm -f "$stderr_file"
   209→
   210→    if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
   211→        # Extract safe response fields for audit logging (no PII)
   212→        local prov_state
   213→        prov_state=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.properties.provisioningState // "accepted"' 2>/dev/null) || prov_state="unknown"
   214→        log "INFO" "${GREEN}SUCCESS: Quota request for $sub_name — model: $model, region: $region, capacity: $capacity (status: $prov_state)${NC}"
   215→        return 0
   216→    else
   217→        # Extract error code and message safely — avoid logging raw response which may contain PII
   218→        local error_code error_msg json_from_cli
   219→        # First: try stdout as JSON (az rest may write JSON body to stdout even on failure)
   220→        error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
   221→        error_msg=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.message // .message // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_msg=""
   222→
   223→        # Second: az rest writes the JSON error body embedded in stderr as "Content: {...}"
   224→        # Extract it and retry jq parsing to surface the real Azure error
   225→        if [[ -z "$error_msg" && -n "$cli_stderr" ]]; then
   226→            json_from_cli=$(echo "$cli_stderr" | sed -n 's/^.*Content: //p' | head -1 2>/dev/null) || json_from_cli=""
   227→            if [[ -z "$json_from_cli" ]]; then
   228→                # Fallback: grab the first JSON-looking token in stderr
   229→                json_from_cli=$(echo "$cli_stderr" | grep -o '{.*}' | head -1 2>/dev/null) || json_from_cli=""
   230→            fi
   231→            if [[ -n "$json_from_cli" ]]; then
   232→                error_code=$(echo "$json_from_cli" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
   233→                error_msg=$(echo "$json_from_cli" | jq -r '.error.message // .message // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_msg=""
   234→            fi
   235→        fi
   236→
   237→        # Third fallback: plain-text az CLI errors (e.g., AADSTS auth errors output as "ERROR: ...")
   238→        if [[ -z "$error_msg" && -n "$cli_stderr" ]]; then
   239→            local plain_line
   240→            plain_line=$(echo "$cli_stderr" | grep -m1 '^ERROR:' | sed 's/^ERROR: //' 2>/dev/null) || plain_line=""
   241→            if [[ -n "$plain_line" ]]; then
   242→                error_msg="$plain_line"
   243→            fi
   244→        fi
   245→
   246→        if [[ -z "$error_msg" ]]; then
   247→            # Last resort: log raw CLI output to file for operator debugging (not shown on console)
   248→            local raw_debug="${cli_stderr:-$response}"
   249→            local timestamp_now
   250→            timestamp_now=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
   251→            echo "${timestamp_now} [DEBUG] Raw az rest output for ${sub_name} (${sub_id}): ${raw_debug:0:500}" >> "$LOG_FILE"
   252→            error_msg="Non-JSON error response from az rest (see log file for details)"
   253→        fi
   254→        local error_context="${error_code:+[$error_code] }${error_msg:-Unknown error}"
   255→        # Truncate to prevent unwieldy log lines from verbose API errors
   256→        error_context="${error_context:0:300}"
   257→        log "ERROR" "${RED}FAILED: Quota request for $sub_name (model: $model, region: $region) - ${error_context}${NC}"
   258→        return 1
   259→    fi
   260→}
   261→
   262→# TUI: Draw the subscription selector menu
   263→draw_menu() {
   264→    local count=${#sub_ids[@]}
   265→    local term_cols
   266→    term_cols=$(tput cols 2>/dev/null || echo 80)
   267→    local max_name_len=$((term_cols - 20))
   268→
   269→    # Move cursor to top of alternate screen and redraw
   270→    # NOTE: In raw mode, \n does not imply \r — use \r\n for proper alignment
   271→    tput cup 0 0
   272→    printf "${BLUE}=== Select Subscriptions ===${NC}\r\n"
   273→    printf "  ↑/↓: navigate  Space: toggle  A: toggle all  Enter: confirm  Q: quit\r\n"
   274→    printf "\r\n"
   275→
   276→    local i=0
   277→    while [[ $i -lt $count ]]; do
   278→        local name="${sub_names[$i]}"
   279→        # Truncate long names
   280→        if [[ ${#name} -gt $max_name_len ]]; then
   281→            name="${name:0:$((max_name_len - 3))}..."
   282→        fi
   283→
   284→        local check=" "
   285→        if [[ "${selected[$i]}" -eq 1 ]]; then
   286→            check="x"
   287→        fi
   288→
   289→        # Clear line before drawing to avoid stale characters
   290→        tput el
   291→        if [[ $i -eq $cursor ]]; then
   292→            tput bold
   293→            printf "  ${BLUE}> [${check}] ${name} (${sub_ids[$i]})${NC}\r\n"
   294→            tput sgr0
   295→        else
   296→            printf "    [${check}] ${name} (${sub_ids[$i]})\r\n"
   297→        fi
   298→        i=$((i + 1))
   299→    done
   300→
   301→    # Count selected
   302→    local sel_count=0
   303→    i=0
   304→    while [[ $i -lt $count ]]; do
   305→        if [[ "${selected[$i]}" -eq 1 ]]; then
   306→            sel_count=$((sel_count + 1))
   307→        fi
   308→        i=$((i + 1))
   309→    done
   310→    printf "\r\n"
   311→    printf "  Selected: ${sel_count} / ${count}\r\n"
   312→}
   313→
   314→# TUI: Handle a single keypress, return 1 on Enter (confirm)
   315→handle_input() {
   316→    local key
   317→    IFS= read -r -s -n1 key
   318→
   319→    case "$key" in
   320→        $'\x1b')
   321→            # Escape sequence — read two more bytes for arrow keys
   322→            local seq1 seq2
   323→            IFS= read -r -s -n1 -t 1 seq1 || true
   324→            IFS= read -r -s -n1 -t 1 seq2 || true
   325→            if [[ "$seq1" == "[" ]]; then
   326→                case "$seq2" in
   327→                    A) # Up
   328→                        if [[ $cursor -gt 0 ]]; then
   329→                            cursor=$((cursor - 1))
   330→                        fi
   331→                        ;;
   332→                    B) # Down
   333→                        if [[ $cursor -lt $((${#sub_ids[@]} - 1)) ]]; then
   334→                            cursor=$((cursor + 1))
   335→                        fi
   336→                        ;;
   337→                esac
   338→            fi
   339→            ;;
   340→        " ")
   341→            # Toggle current selection
   342→            if [[ "${selected[cursor]}" -eq 1 ]]; then
   343→                selected[cursor]=0
   344→            else
   345→                selected[cursor]=1
   346→            fi
   347→            ;;
   348→        a|A)
   349→            # Toggle all
   350→            local all_selected=1
   351→            local i=0
   352→            while [[ $i -lt ${#sub_ids[@]} ]]; do
   353→                if [[ "${selected[$i]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
   354→                    all_selected=0
   355→                    break
   356→                fi
   357→                i=$((i + 1))
   358→            done
   359→            local new_val=1
   360→            if [[ $all_selected -eq 1 ]]; then
   361→                new_val=0
   362→            fi
   363→            i=0
   364→            while [[ $i -lt ${#sub_ids[@]} ]]; do
   365→                selected[i]=$new_val
   366→                i=$((i + 1))
   367→            done
   368→            ;;
   369→        ""|$'\n')
   370→            # Enter — confirm
   371→            return 1
   372→            ;;
   373→        q|Q)
   374→            # Quit — exit alternate screen before logging
   375→            tput rmcup 2>/dev/null || true
   376→            tput cnorm 2>/dev/null || true
   377→            tput sgr0 2>/dev/null || true
   378→            if [[ -n "${saved_tty:-}" ]]; then
   379→                stty "$saved_tty" 2>/dev/null || true
   380→            fi
   381→            trap - EXIT
   382→            log "INFO" "${YELLOW}Cancelled by user${NC}"
   383→            exit 0
   384→            ;;
   385→    esac
   386→    return 0
   387→}
   388→
   389→# TUI: Interactive subscription selector
   390→# Populates selected_indices array with chosen subscription indices
   391→show_subscription_selector() {
   392→    local subs_json=$1
   393→
   394→    # Skip TUI if stdin is not a terminal (pipe/CI)
   395→    if [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then
   396→        log "INFO" "Non-interactive mode detected — processing all subscriptions"
   397→        return 0
   398→    fi
   399→
   400→    # Populate parallel indexed arrays
   401→    sub_ids=()
   402→    sub_names=()
   403→    selected=()
   404→    cursor=0
   405→
   406→    local i=0
   407→    while IFS= read -r line; do
   408→        sub_ids+=("$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.id')")
   409→        sub_names+=("$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.name')")
   410→        selected+=(1)
   411→        i=$((i + 1))
   412→    done < <(echo "$subs_json" | jq -c '.[]')
   413→
   414→    local count=${#sub_ids[@]}
   415→    if [[ $count -eq 0 ]]; then
   416→        return 0
   417→    fi
   418→
   419→    # Save terminal state and enter raw mode
   420→    saved_tty=$(stty -g 2>/dev/null) || true
   421→    # shellcheck disable=SC2064
   422→    trap "tput rmcup 2>/dev/null; tput cnorm 2>/dev/null; tput sgr0 2>/dev/null; [[ -n \"\${saved_tty:-}\" ]] && stty \"\$saved_tty\" 2>/dev/null; echo" EXIT
   423→    tput smcup 2>/dev/null || true
   424→    stty -echo raw 2>/dev/null || true
   425→    tput civis 2>/dev/null || true
   426→
   427→    # Main TUI loop
   428→    while true; do
   429→        draw_menu
   430→        handle_input || break
   431→    done
   432→
   433→    # Restore terminal: exit alternate screen, show cursor, reset attributes
   434→    tput rmcup 2>/dev/null || true
   435→    tput cnorm 2>/dev/null || true
   436→    tput sgr0 2>/dev/null || true
   437→    if [[ -n "${saved_tty:-}" ]]; then
   438→        stty "$saved_tty" 2>/dev/null || true
   439→    fi
   440→    # Remove EXIT trap (restore default)
   441→    trap - EXIT
   442→
   443→    # Build selected_indices array
   444→    selected_indices=()
   445→    i=0
   446→    while [[ $i -lt $count ]]; do
   447→        if [[ "${selected[$i]}" -eq 1 ]]; then
   448→            selected_indices+=("$i")
   449→        fi
   450→        i=$((i + 1))
   451→    done
   452→
   453→    return 0
   454→}
   455→
   456→# Main execution
   457→main() {
   458→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}=== Azure AI Quota Request Automation ===${NC}"
   459→    log "INFO" "Starting batch quota request submission"
   460→    
   461→    # Check dependencies
   462→    check_dependencies
   463→
   464→    # Verify Azure CLI authentication (FR17)
   465→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}Verifying Azure CLI authentication...${NC}"
   466→    local auth_err
   467→    auth_err=$(az account show 2>&1) || error_exit "Azure CLI session is not authenticated. Run 'az login' first. Details: $auth_err"
   468→    log "INFO" "${GREEN}Azure CLI authentication verified${NC}"
   469→
   470→    # Validate configuration
   471→    validate_config
   472→    
   473→    # Read configuration
   474→    local config
   475→    config=$(cat "$CONFIG_FILE")
   476→
   477→    # Discover subscriptions from Azure account
   478→    local subs_json
   479→    subs_json=$(discover_subscriptions)
   480→
   481→    local sub_count
   482→    sub_count=$(echo "$subs_json" | jq 'length')
   483→
   484→    # Subscription selection: --all flag bypasses TUI
   485→    selected_indices=()
   486→    if [[ "$PROCESS_ALL" == "true" ]]; then
   487→        log "INFO" "Processing all discovered subscriptions (--all flag)"
   488→        local idx=0
   489→        while [[ $idx -lt $sub_count ]]; do
   490→            selected_indices+=("$idx")
   491→            idx=$((idx + 1))
   492→        done
   493→    else
   494→        show_subscription_selector "$subs_json"
   495→
   496→        # If TUI was skipped (non-interactive), select all indices
   497→        if [[ ${#selected_indices[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   498→            local idx=0
   499→            while [[ $idx -lt $sub_count ]]; do
   500→                selected_indices+=("$idx")
   501→                idx=$((idx + 1))
   502→            done
   503→        fi
   504→    fi
   505→
   506→    local selected_count=${#selected_indices[@]}
   507→
   508→    if [[ "$selected_count" -eq 0 ]]; then
   509→        log "WARN" "${YELLOW}No subscriptions selected — nothing to process${NC}"
   510→        exit 0
   511→    fi
   512→
   513→    log "INFO" "Processing $selected_count of $sub_count subscription(s)"
   514→
   515→    # Build jq index filter for selected subscriptions
   516→    local jq_filter
   517→    jq_filter=$(printf ",%s" "${selected_indices[@]}")
   518→    jq_filter="[.[${jq_filter:1}]][]"
   519→
   520→    # Process each selected subscription (process substitution keeps loop in main shell)
   521→    local success_count=0
   522→    local failed_count=0
   523→    local sub_id
   524→    local sub_name
   525→    local retry_count
   526→
   527→    while read -r sub; do
   528→        sub_id=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.id')
   529→        sub_name=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.name')
   530→
   531→        # Validate subscription fields before processing
   532→        if [[ -z "$sub_id" || "$sub_id" == "null" || -z "$sub_name" || "$sub_name" == "null" ]]; then
   533→            log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Skipping malformed subscription entry (missing id or name)${NC}"
   534→            failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
   535→            continue
   536→        fi
   537→
   538→        # Try to submit quota request with retries
   539→        retry_count=0
   540→        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
   541→            if submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$config"; then
   542→                success_count=$((success_count + 1))
   543→                break
   544→            else
   545→                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
   546→                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
   547→                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
   548→                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
   549→                else
   550→                    failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
   551→                    log "ERROR" "${RED}Failed to submit quota request for $sub_name after $MAX_RETRIES attempts${NC}"
   552→                fi
   553→            fi
   554→        done
   555→    done < <(echo "$subs_json" | jq -c "$jq_filter")
   556→
   557→    # Summary
   558→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}=== Submission Summary ===${NC}"
   559→    log "INFO" "Total subscriptions processed: $selected_count"
   560→    log "INFO" "${GREEN}Successful: $success_count${NC}"
   561→    log "INFO" "${RED}Failed: $failed_count${NC}"
   562→    log "INFO" "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
   563→
   564→    # Exit with non-zero code if any subscriptions failed (FR14)
   565→    if [[ $failed_count -gt 0 ]]; then
   566→        exit 1
   567→    fi
   568→}
   569→
   570→# Run main function
   571→main "$@"
   572→
